auditor.summary: The build implements the core mobile responsive requirements: hamburger menu with slide-in drawer, responsive KPI grid, table horizontal scroll wrapper, and touch target utilities. However, there are several issues: (1) the global table CSS rule may break non-dashboard pages, (2) the font-size floor implementation may not override explicit Tailwind font-size classes, (3) desktop sidebar nav links lack touch-target class, (4) potential desktop layout regression from padding changes, and (5) performance cannot be verified without testing. The build is recoverable with fixes but requires attention before shipping.

top_issues.description: Desktop sidebar navigation links do not have 'touch-target' class. While desktop may not require 44px touch targets, the acceptance criterion states 'All interactive elements (buttons, nav links, hamburger) have a bounding box >= 44x44px at 393px viewport'. At 393px, the desktop sidebar is hidden, but the criterion doesn't specify viewport-dependent targeting. If interpreted strictly, all interactive elements everywhere must meet 44px minimum.

The font-size floor rule 'p, span, a, li, h1, h2, h3, h4, h5, h6, button, label, input, textarea, select { font-size: max(14px, inherit); }' uses 'inherit' which may not override explicit Tailwind font-size classes like 'text-xs' (12px). The 'max()' function with 'inherit' evaluates to the inherited value, not the explicitly set value. This means elements with 'text-xs' class will still render at 12px.

Global 'table { display: block; overflow-x: auto; }' rule applies to ALL tables in the application, not just dashboard tables. This could break tables on non-dashboard pages (e.g., RFQ pages, login page) by forcing them into block display with horizontal scroll. The spec intended this only for dashboard tables.
